verdict.note: Real browser (CDP over cached Chromium) against live backend: right-click a live row → menu ['rename','attach…','select…','close']; attach opens modal titled by headline showing 'spex session attach ee71187c-…' (correct id), read-only + click-to-select + copy button. CLI verb itself verified attaching via pty.

Real browser (CDP/Chromium) via vite→worktree backend (tmuxSocket sourced from /api/settings). Modal shows two labeled rows: spex 'spex session attach 0a5d5187…' and tmux 'tmux -L spexcode attach -t 0a5d5187…', each read-only, click-to-select, own copy button. Both commands verified attaching to the live tmux via pty (spex verb + raw tmux).

Real browser (CDP) via vite→live backend. Created a throwaway session, stopped it to OFFLINE, then drove the real right-click menu on both: LIVE row menu = [rename, attach…, select…, close] (attach PRESENT); OFFLINE row menu = [rename, select…, close] (attach ABSENT). Screenshot shows the offline row's menu with no attach item.

Re-measured after the label change (real browser, CDP): the menu now reads [rename, 'open in terminal…', select…, close]; picking it opens the same modal with both rows — spex 'spex session attach <id>' and tmux 'tmux -L spexcode attach -t <id>', each read-only + copy. Label is friendlier; modal contents unchanged.

Re-measured after the label reword (real browser, CDP): throwaway session stopped to OFFLINE. LIVE row menu = [rename, 'open in terminal…', select…, close] (item PRESENT); OFFLINE row menu = [rename, select…, close] (ABSENT). Gate behaviour unchanged; confirms the new label.
Live DOM has no instructional paragraph; both attach commands and copy buttons remain.
Real browser: live row menu included open in terminal; the same row omitted it after becoming offline.
